## 2026-04-03 — Procurement Summary Derives A Stale External Share Queue

- shipped
  - the admin/location procurement quality summary now derives a dedicated `외부 공유 재확인 queue` from stale council-backed `share.create` audit entries
  - queue items are keyed by `project_id + share_project_document_id` and preserve the latest stale external share per project document
  - the `외부 공유 review (N)` preset now follows that same unique queue count instead of raw event volume, while the queue header still shows cumulative stale `share.create` event count as a separate context metric
  - the queue surfaces:
    - project/document linkage
    - latest external share timestamp
    - latest stale-share actor and stale-share count
    - live share-link state (`share_id`, public `share_url`, active/inactive, access count, last public access, expiry)
    - council freshness drift copy (`stale_procurement`, `stale_revision`)
    - the same stale-share summary text already shown in recent activity
  - active public links now sort ahead of inactive or missing-record links, and the queue header breaks out active/inactive/missing-record counts so operators can prioritize live external exposure first
  - active stale links with recorded public access now sort ahead of active-but-unopened links, and the queue header breaks those counts apart as `최근 public 열람 있음` vs `아직 열람 없음`
  - when any active stale public link remains, the procurement summary modal now raises a top-level exposure alert with an `외부 공유 review 열기` CTA that activates the existing stale-share preset directly
  - the locations overview card now consumes `/admin/locations?include_procurement=1` and surfaces the same active stale-share exposure as a compact risk strip, including a direct `외부 공유 review` CTA before the operator opens the full procurement modal
  - the locations overview snapshot now comes from a dedicated stale-share aggregation path instead of rebuilding the full procurement summary per tenant card, while keeping the same `include_procurement=1` contract
  - the locations overview grid now also sorts tenants with active stale public exposure ahead of normal tenants, and among exposed tenants it lifts recently accessed stale public links first
  - the locations overview card now also surfaces the top stale shared document with live share-link state plus direct `공유 링크 열기` and `공유 링크 복사` actions
  - the same card now also surfaces the latest stale-share actor, timestamp, and cumulative stale-share count for that top document, so operator handoff does not require opening the modal first
  - the same card now also exposes `외부 공유 review 링크`, which copies the tenant-wide stale-share review URL with the existing stale-share activity filter preserved
  - active stale public links can now be revoked directly from the locations overview card and the stale-share review modal by reusing the existing `DELETE /share/{share_id}` flow, and admin operators are allowed to revoke stale links even when another user created them
  - the same card now also exposes `위험 문서 review`, which opens the summary already focused on the top stale shared project while keeping the stale-share activity filter active
  - the same card now also exposes `위험 문서 review 링크`, which copies the exact internal procurement-summary review URL with tenant, focused project, and stale-share filter preserved
  - focused project cards now also expose the latest stale external share item when present
  - the queue intentionally reuses only existing actions:
    - project open
    - current review link copy
    - shared link open
    - shared link copy
- file path
  - `app/routers/admin.py`
  - `app/static/index.html`
  - `tests/test_tenant.py`
  - `tests/e2e/test_main_flow.py`
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - the new preset made stale external shares easy to isolate, but operators still had to scan raw recent events to reconstruct which exact project document was last shared on an outdated council basis
  - deriving a compact queue closes that gap without introducing another persistence model or widening the share workflow
- validation
  - `PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/decisiondoc-pycache python3 -m py_compile app/routers/admin.py tests/test_tenant.py`
    - `pass`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_tenant.py -k 'procurement_quality_summary and stale_share' --tb=short`
    - `pass`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/e2e/test_main_flow.py -k 'stale_share_review or locations_page_shows_procurement_quality_summary or location_procurement_summary_blocked_event_retries_after_override_reason' --tb=short`
    - `pass`

    - `1 passed, 37 deselected`

## 2026-04-04 — Local Stale-Share Demo Seed For Direct Operator Verification

- shipped
  - a dedicated local helper script now seeds one deterministic stale-share demo into a fresh `DATA_DIR`
  - the helper creates:
    - one system admin login
    - one council-backed `bid_decision_kr` project document
    - one later procurement update that makes the saved council stale
    - one active stale public share with one recorded public access
    - one clean contrast tenant so the locations overview risk ordering is visible immediately
  - the helper prints:
    - seeded login credentials
    - tenant-wide stale-share review URL
    - focused stale-share review URL
    - exact public `/shared/{id}` URL
  - a companion verifier script now checks a running local app against that seeded state:
    - admin login
    - locations overview stale-share exposure
    - focused stale-share procurement summary
    - stale Decision Council binding
    - public shared-page warning
  - a one-command launcher now starts local `uvicorn`, waits for `/health`, runs the seed helper, runs the verifier, and optionally keeps the app alive for manual checks
  - the launcher now also writes a deterministic demo manifest into the chosen `DATA_DIR` and can optionally open the focused internal review URL plus the exact public share URL in a browser
  - the helper intentionally rejects a non-empty `DATA_DIR` because append-only audit/share state would otherwise make the stale-share counts noisy and non-deterministic
- file path
  - `scripts/seed_procurement_stale_share_demo.py`
  - `scripts/check_procurement_stale_share_demo.py`
  - `scripts/run_procurement_stale_share_demo.py`
  - `tests/test_procurement_demo_seed.py`
  - `tests/test_procurement_demo_verify.py`
  - `tests/test_procurement_demo_run.py`
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/IMPLEMENT.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - the stale-share review flow was already closed inside the product, but local reproduction still required manual store edits or brittle ad-hoc setup
  - adding one deterministic seed helper lets operators and reviewers boot a fresh local demo and verify the real UI flow directly without widening the product surface

## 2026-04-04 — Decision Council v1 Proposal-First Expansion Closed

- shipped
  - the stored canonical procurement council session remains keyed to `target_bundle_type=bid_decision_kr`, but the same latest session is now reused for both:
    - `bid_decision_kr`
    - `proposal_kr`
  - `GET /projects/{project_id}/decision-council` now exposes `supported_bundle_types=["bid_decision_kr","proposal_kr"]`
  - generation metadata and audit detail now distinguish:
    - the stored council source bundle via `decision_council_target_bundle=bid_decision_kr`
    - the actual applied downstream bundle via `decision_council_applied_bundle`
  - current procurement binding validation remains unchanged:
    - `opportunity + recommendation` must exist
    - `source_procurement_decision_id` must still match
    - `source_procurement_updated_at` must still match
  - if that binding is stale, both `bid_decision_kr` and `proposal_kr` generation now skip council handoff with:
    - `decision_council_handoff_used=false`
    - `decision_council_handoff_skipped_reason=stale_procurement_context`
  - the project-detail procurement panel now treats Decision Council as `v1` and exposes proposal-first generation alongside the existing bid-decision path
  - council-backed `proposal_kr` project documents now reuse the same freshness contract, stale warnings, follow-up CTA, approval/share confirm guard, modal warning, public shared-page warning, and stale external share triage path as council-backed `bid_decision_kr`
  - stale external share review and admin overview now preserve bundle-level labeling so operators can distinguish `의사결정 문서` from `제안서` without creating a new queue model
  - the optional procurement smoke lane now validates proposal-first council provenance and the local OpenSpace bridge remains docs/skill scaffolding only; no runtime OpenSpace dependency was introduced
  - request-level audit for `/generate/stream` is now deferred until the stream finishes so stale council skip reason and applied-bundle metadata are preserved in the primary `doc.generate` entry without duplicating generate counts
  - the share HTML regression test now reads from the app-scoped share store path instead of a bare default store, which makes full-suite execution stable under mixed `DATA_DIR` test runs
- file path
  - `app/schemas.py`
  - `app/storage/decision_council_store.py`
  - `app/services/decision_council_service.py`
  - `app/services/generation_service.py`
  - `app/routers/generate.py`
  - `app/middleware/audit.py`
  - `app/routers/admin.py`
  - `app/routers/projects.py`

  - `tests/test_tenant.py`
  - `tests/test_smoke_script.py`
  - `tests/test_phase3_features.py`
  - `tests/e2e/test_main_flow.py`

  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/QUALITY.md`

  - `.agents/skills/decisiondoc-openspace-docgen/SKILL.md`
  - `.agents/skills/decisiondoc-openspace-eval/SKILL.md`
- reason for change
  - Decision Council v0 closed the pre-generation decision step for `bid_decision_kr`, but proposal drafting still depended only on generic procurement handoff and lacked the same provenance, stale guard, and operator review visibility
  - extending the existing canonical session to `proposal_kr` preserves the current project/approval/share/export model while closing the actual downstream operator gap
  - the stream audit fix was required because proposal-first observability would otherwise look complete in structured logs but remain missing in persisted audit records

  - this helper collapses that into one local command while keeping the existing `scripts/smoke.py` contract unchanged

## 2026-04-06 — Prod Deploy-Smoke Lambda AccessDenied Narrowed And Artifact Bucket Path Hardened

- shipped
  - reran `deploy-smoke` on the latest merged `main` after PR #17 and confirmed the workflow reached `SAM deploy` before failing, so the remaining blocker is no longer local CI, local smoke helpers, or application test coverage
  - inspected the live prod stack, Lambda function configuration, deploy role policy, artifact objects, and CloudTrail management events to separate application regressions from infrastructure-path failures
  - updated `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml` so `sam deploy` now uploads artifacts into the stage-owned `DECISIONDOC_S3_BUCKET_<STAGE>` under `sam-artifacts/<stage>/` instead of the SAM CLI managed default source bucket
  - documented the same mitigation and the required stack recovery command for environments already left in `UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED`
- file path
  - `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml`
  - `docs/deploy_aws.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - GitHub Actions run `24017366147` failed at `SAM deploy`, and CloudFormation showed `DecisionDocFunction` update failing with Lambda `AccessDeniedException` before any post-deploy smoke ran
  - live inspection showed the GitHub OIDC deploy role still has broad `cloudformation:*`, `iam:*`, `lambda:*`, and `s3:*` permissions, the prod Lambda execution role trust is still valid, and both the previous and newly uploaded SAM artifacts exist in S3 with `AES256` object encryption
  - given that evidence, the smallest practical mitigation is to remove one more external dependency from deploy time and pin artifact uploads to the stage-owned bucket already managed by the application stack
- validation
  - `gh api 'repos/sungjin9288/DecisionDoc-AI/actions/runs/24017366147/jobs'`
    - `deploy` failed, `smoke` skipped
  - `gh api -i 'repos/sungjin9288/DecisionDoc-AI/actions/jobs/70039282183/logs'`
    - `SAM deploy` failed while CloudFormation was updating `DecisionDocFunction`
  - `aws cloudformation describe-stack-events --stack-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2 --max-items 20`
    - stack status confirmed as `UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED`
  - `aws lambda get-function-configuration --function-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2`
    - current prod Lambda still reflects the March 28 successful deployment
  - `aws iam get-role-policy --role-name decisiondoc-github-actions-deploy-prod --policy-name decisiondoc-github-actions-deploy-prod`
    - deploy role still allows broad CloudFormation/Lambda/S3 actions
  - `aws s3api head-object --bucket aws-sam-cli-managed-default-samclisourcebucket-gy2eizrxryln --key f9f70fe09e95717d845a21b5e66154a9`
    - previous artifact exists
  - `aws s3api head-object --bucket aws-sam-cli-managed-default-samclisourcebucket-gy2eizrxryln --key 6234372955d514664bcd7d42961d14cd`
    - failing-run artifact also exists
- remaining boundary
  - prod stack is currently `UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED`, so the next live rerun still needs:
    - `aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback --stack-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2`
  - after rollback recovery, `deploy-smoke` must be rerun from the latest merged `main` to verify the stage-bucket artifact path actually clears the prior Lambda update failure

## 2026-04-06 — Prod Lambda Update Blocker Classified As AWS-Side Restriction And Week-1 Operating Docs Added

- shipped
  - added long-term operating guidance in `docs/operating_model_roadmap.md` with a concrete 4-week execution plan centered on environment separation, stage-first release discipline, immutable deploy preference, and smoke/operator isolation
  - updated `docs/deploy_aws.md` with a deploy ownership map, prod rerun gate, and explicit recovery/diagnostic commands so operators stop treating repeated `deploy-smoke` reruns as a normal incident response
  - updated `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md` so production deployment now calls out ownership boundaries, rerun stop conditions, and the known `DecisionDocFunction` access-denied pattern
- file path
  - `docs/operating_model_roadmap.md`
  - `docs/deploy_aws.md`
  - `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - after the stage-owned artifact bucket mitigation landed, the remaining production blocker was still `DecisionDocFunction` update failure with Lambda `AccessDeniedException`
  - the next question was no longer “how to rerun deploy-smoke” but “how to prevent operators from repeatedly using the wrong recovery move while the underlying AWS restriction remains unresolved”
  - a durable operating model was needed because the repo is now large enough that deployment discipline, smoke identity separation, and environment roles matter as much as feature correctness
- validation
  - `aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2 --query 'Stacks[0].[StackStatus,StackStatusReason]' --output text`
    - stack failure remained isolated to `DecisionDocFunction`
  - `aws iam get-role-policy --role-name decisiondoc-github-actions-deploy-prod --policy-name decisiondoc-github-actions-deploy-prod`
    - deploy role inline policy still included broad `lambda:*`, `s3:*`, and `cloudformation:*`
  - `aws iam simulate-principal-policy --policy-source-arn arn:aws:iam::217139788460:role/decisiondoc-github-actions-deploy-prod --action-names lambda:UpdateFunctionCode ...`
    - IAM simulation result was `allowed`
  - `aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name community_`
    - local admin user had `AdministratorAccess`
  - `aws iam simulate-principal-policy --policy-source-arn arn:aws:iam::217139788460:user/community_ --action-names lambda:UpdateFunctionCode ...`
    - local admin IAM simulation result was also `allowed`
  - `aws lambda update-function-code --function-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2 --s3-bucket decisiondoc-ai-prod-217139788460-apne2 --s3-key sam-artifacts/prod//d2ee02e06f0045f866cc6eb4efd0ce26 --dry-run`
    - still failed with `AccessDeniedException`
  - `aws lambda update-function-code --function-name decisiondoc-ai-prod --region ap-northeast-2 --zip-file fileb://... --dry-run`
    - inline zip dry-run also failed with `AccessDeniedException`
  - `aws organizations list-policies-for-target --target-id 217139788460 --filter SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY`
    - returned `AWSOrganizationsNotInUseException`, so SCP was ruled out
  - `git diff --check -- README.md docs/deploy_aws.md docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md docs/operating_model_roadmap.md docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
    - pass
- remaining boundary
  - current evidence points away from repo code, workflow artifact path, ordinary IAM allow, or Organizations SCP
  - the remaining blocker is an AWS-side Lambda update restriction that still prevents `UpdateFunctionCode`, even for a local admin user with `AdministratorAccess`
  - until that restriction is resolved, `prod deploy-smoke` reruns should remain paused and `prod` should be treated as a recovery/diagnosis target rather than a feature-validation environment

## 2026-04-06 — Deploy-Smoke Fail-Fast Guard Added For Broken Prod Stack And Lambda Dry-Run Deny

- shipped
  - updated `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml` so the deploy job now checks stack mutability before `SAM build` and `SAM deploy`
  - the new preflight stops immediately when the target stack is already `UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED`
  - the same preflight also runs `aws lambda update-function-code --dry-run` against the target function and fails fast when Lambda code update is blocked
  - aligned `docs/deploy_aws.md` and `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md` with the same fail-fast semantics
- file path
  - `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml`
  - `docs/deploy_aws.md`
  - `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - the current prod failure mode is not a normal application regression but an AWS-side Lambda update restriction, and repeated `deploy-smoke` reruns only recreate `UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED`
  - a release workflow should surface that state before trying `SAM deploy`, not after another failed stack update
- validation
  - `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml docs/deploy_aws.md docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`

    - planned as touched-area regression gate for deploy/procurement smoke helpers
- remaining boundary
  - this guardrail reduces operator error and repeated failed deploy attempts, but it does not resolve the underlying AWS-side Lambda update restriction
  - production redeploy still remains blocked until that AWS-side restriction is cleared

  - added optional `workflow_dispatch` input `break_glass_reason` so true incident recovery can still override the gate with an explicit operator reason
  - aligned `docs/deploy_aws.md`, `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md`, and `docs/operating_model_roadmap.md` with the new stage-first semantics

  - the Week 2 operating-model goal was to make stage-first promotion a workflow-enforced contract, not just a documentation preference

## 2026-04-06 — Fresh-Stack Deployment Suffix Added For Lambda Update Restriction Workaround

- shipped
  - added optional `DeploymentSuffix` support to `infra/sam/template.yaml` so Lambda function names can move from `decisiondoc-ai-<stage>` to `decisiondoc-ai-<stage><suffix>` without changing the default path
  - updated `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` and `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml` so manual dispatch can supply `deployment_suffix` and deploy a separate stack/function pair instead of overwriting the currently blocked stage stack
  - made the `prod` dev-first gate suffix-aware, so `prod` with `deployment_suffix=-green` now requires a successful `deploy-smoke [dev-green]` run for the same `main` SHA
  - documented the same fresh-stack workaround in deploy/prod operating docs
- file path
  - `infra/sam/template.yaml`
  - `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml`
  - `docs/deploy_aws.md`
  - `docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md`
  - `docs/operating_model_roadmap.md`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - `decisiondoc-ai-dev` and `decisiondoc-ai-prod` both now fail `aws lambda update-function-code --dry-run` with `AccessDeniedException`, so normal in-place update is blocked before any application smoke can run
  - the next smallest repo-level mitigation is to support a fresh stack/function name so deployment can test whether `CreateFunction` or new-stack provisioning is still allowed even while updates to the old function stay denied
  - because `prod` promotion discipline must survive this workaround too, the same suffix has to propagate into the dev-first release gate rather than creating an untracked side path
- validation
  - planned local verification:
    - `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/deploy.yml"); YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml"); puts "yaml-ok"'`
    - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_stage_procurement_smoke_run.py tests/test_export_stage_procurement_smoke_env.py tests/test_smoke_script.py --tb=short`
    - `git diff --check -- infra/sam/template.yaml .github/workflows/deploy.yml .github/workflows/deploy-smoke.yml docs/deploy_aws.md docs/deployment/prod_checklist.md docs/operating_model_roadmap.md docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- remaining boundary
  - this workaround only creates a fresh naming path; it does not prove that the AWS account will allow a new `CreateFunction` or new stack create
  - the next live check still has to be `deploy-smoke [dev-green]` or a similar suffixed `dev` run before any suffixed `prod` promotion is considered

## 2026-04-16 — Procurement PDF Normalization Added To Attachment/RFP Parsing

- shipped
  - added `app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py` to turn structured PDF extraction into a procurement-oriented summary block with document type guess, key sections, procurement signals, PPT candidate pages, and review notes
  - updated `POST /generate/with-attachments`, `POST /generate/from-documents`, and `POST /attachments/parse-rfp` so PDF attachments prepend this normalized context before the raw extracted text
  - kept the change additive and PDF-only so existing non-PDF attachment flows, provider boundaries, and storage behavior stay unchanged
- file path
  - `app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py`
  - `app/services/rfp_parser.py`
  - `app/routers/generate.py`
  - `tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py`
  - `tests/test_rfp_parsing.py`
  - `tests/test_generate_from_documents.py`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - real public-sector kickoff/evaluation PDFs were readable but still too flat for downstream generation because raw text alone did not preserve heading hierarchy, procurement signals, or slide-worthy structure
  - the smallest safe improvement was to reuse the existing structured PDF extraction and add a procurement-specific normalization layer rather than rewriting attachment parsing or provider prompts
- validation
  - `python3 -m py_compile app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py app/services/rfp_parser.py app/routers/generate.py tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py tests/test_rfp_parsing.py tests/test_generate_from_documents.py`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py tests/test_rfp_parsing.py tests/test_generate_from_documents.py tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py --tb=short`
- remaining boundary
  - this improves generation inputs for procurement PDFs, but it does not yet classify embedded images/diagrams or fully reconstruct multi-column slide layouts from the source PDF

## 2026-04-16 — Procurement PDF Page Classifier Added For Better PPT/Section Planning

- shipped
  - extended `extract_pdf_structured()` so structured PDF extraction now returns per-page summaries with page number, detected headings, preview text, and table presence
  - upgraded `app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py` to classify pages into document-oriented buckets such as `개요/배경`, `평가기준/지표`, `일정/마일스톤`, `추진절차/방법`, and `조직/거버넌스`
  - added a new `페이지 분류:` block and made `발표/PPT 후보 페이지:` prefer page-classifier output over heading-only heuristics when page metadata is available
- file path
  - `app/services/attachment_service.py`
  - `app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py`
  - `tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py`
  - `tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - heading-only normalization improved procurement PDF ingestion but still mixed true section headings with fragmented legal/body text on real kickoff and evaluation slides
  - the next smallest safe step was to add page-level structure so downstream generation can distinguish `개요`, `평가기준`, `일정`, and `거버넌스` pages even when heading extraction is imperfect
- validation
  - `python3 -m py_compile app/services/attachment_service.py app/services/procurement_pdf_normalizer.py tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py tests/test_rfp_parsing.py tests/test_generate_from_documents.py tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py --tb=short`
- remaining boundary
  - this page classifier improves slide/chapter planning inputs, but it still does not classify embedded images/diagrams or fully recover multi-column slide layouts

## 2026-04-16 — Prompt Contract Updated To Use Procurement PDF Slide Hints In slide_outline

- shipped
  - updated `build_bundle_prompt()` so bundles that declare `slide_outline` now receive an explicit `공공조달 PPT 설계 적용 규칙` block when procurement context is present
  - the prompt now tells the model to treat `페이지 분류`, `PPT 페이지 설계 힌트`, and `발표/PPT 후보 페이지` as source-of-truth inputs for `slide_outline.title`, `core_message`, `evidence_points`, `visual_type`, `visual_brief`, and `layout_hint`
- file path
  - `app/domain/schema.py`
  - `tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - procurement PDF normalization and page classification were already being injected into context, but the prompt still left it implicit whether the model must use those hints when composing slide-by-slide structure
  - the next minimal improvement was to make the contract explicit instead of relying on the model to infer that relationship on its own
- validation
  - `python3 -m py_compile app/domain/schema.py tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_procurement_pdf_normalizer.py tests/test_rfp_parsing.py tests/test_generate_from_documents.py tests/test_pdf_enhanced.py --tb=short`
- remaining boundary
  - this strengthens prompt grounding, but it does not yet enforce post-generation validation that every `slide_outline` item actually matches the classified procurement pages

## 2026-04-16 — Knowledge Reference Ranking Added For Better Learning-From-Examples Flow

- shipped
  - extended project knowledge documents with reusable learning metadata such as `learning_mode`, `quality_tier`, `applicable_bundles`, `source_organization`, `reference_year`, `success_state`, and `notes`
  - updated the knowledge API so uploads can persist those fields, metadata can be edited after upload, and context preview can show ranked reference documents for a target bundle/title/goal
  - changed generation-time project knowledge injection to rank documents by approval state, quality tier, bundle match, and query overlap instead of pure recency so approved examples are prioritized during bundle generation
- file path
  - `app/storage/knowledge_store.py`
  - `app/routers/knowledge.py`
  - `app/services/generation_service.py`
  - `app/schemas.py`
  - `tests/test_knowledge.py`
  - `tests/test_generate.py`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - project knowledge existed, but it still behaved like a flat attachment bucket because generation consumed the newest files first regardless of whether they were approved winning outputs or bundle-specific references
  - the next smallest useful step was to let operators mark which documents are gold references and have generation prefer those documents automatically without adding a new storage system or model-training path
- validation
  - `python3 -m py_compile app/storage/knowledge_store.py app/routers/knowledge.py app/services/generation_service.py app/schemas.py tests/test_knowledge.py tests/test_generate.py`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_knowledge.py tests/test_generate.py --tb=short`
- remaining boundary
  - this adds reference-library behavior and ranking, but it does not yet implement a full review UI for metadata management or an automatic feedback loop that promotes user-edited outputs into approved examples

## 2026-04-16 — Approved Output Promotion Added To Close The Learning Loop

- shipped
  - added `POST /knowledge/{project_id}/promote-generated` so approved generated docs can be promoted into project knowledge as `approved_output` references without re-uploading files manually
  - promotion automatically tags the new knowledge entries with the originating `bundle_type`, keeps optional `source_bundle_id` and `source_request_id` in the API response, and stores the promoted markdown with `approved_output` learning metadata for future ranking
- file path
  - `app/routers/knowledge.py`
  - `app/schemas.py`
  - `tests/test_knowledge.py`
  - `docs/specs/public_procurement_copilot/STATUS.md`
- reason for change
  - reference ranking alone is not enough if operators must manually repackage every approved final output before the system can learn from it
  - the smallest practical next step was to let the product promote finalized docs directly into the same knowledge store that generation already consumes
- validation
  - `python3 -m py_compile app/routers/knowledge.py app/schemas.py tests/test_knowledge.py`
  - `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_knowledge.py tests/test_generate.py --tb=short`
- remaining boundary
  - this closes the backend promotion path, but it still needs a dedicated UI action so approved outputs can be promoted without calling the API directly

## 2026-06-24 — Procurement Decision Package Validation Consolidated Around Writer Boundary

- shipped
  - consolidated decision-package contract validation through `validate_package_document()` so writer output, sample validation, and artifact checking share the same package-level rules
  - kept `scripts/validate_procurement_decision_package_sample.py` focused on sample-specific guarantees: sample ID/date matching, opportunity reference parity, non-empty local demo coverage, and required source/missing-evidence coverage
  - simplified artifact checking so split JSON files are validated through the same package contract and must match their source sections in `decision_package.json`
  - centralized the split JSON artifact-to-package-field map so artifact writing and artifact checking use the same ordered source of truth
  - centralized local demo coverage validation so sample validation and artifact checking share one rule for non-empty filters, score factors, source/missing evidence coverage, and checklist items
  - made sample input validation reuse the service-level demo input validator, leaving only fixture-specific buyer/profile/operator field checks in the sample validator
  - moved expected-package-to-sample parity validation into the service layer so the sample validator no longer repeats top-level package ordering, schema purpose, timestamp, opportunity reference, and local coverage checks
  - added a path-aware package validator wrapper so sample validation and artifact checking reuse the same package contract while preserving reader-facing error paths
  - moved split artifact section validation into the service layer so artifact checking no longer hand-builds temporary package documents or rewrites validator paths locally
  - centralized artifact inventory fingerprint building and validation in the service layer so demo execution and artifact checking use one evidence contract for SHA256, size, artifact keys, and artifact order
  - centralized demo artifact-list validation in the service layer so `demo_run_result.artifacts` uses the same artifact order contract and checker no longer owns package-artifact missing/unknown/order rules
  - centralized demo receipt artifact-row rendering and receipt-text validation in the service layer so demo execution and artifact checking use the same receipt table and validation-summary contract
  - centralized demo evidence receipt file/path validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer owns receipt path and existence checks
  - centralized demo evidence result, inventory, and receipt file validation in the service layer so artifact checking only merges the returned evidence-check flags
  - moved the demo evidence receipt filename into the service layer so demo execution and artifact checking share the same receipt artifact name
  - centralized markdown artifact text validation for sign-off summary, decision summary, and evidence summary so artifact checking reuses service-owned boundary and evidence marker contracts
  - split decision summary hard-filter and next-action markdown rendering into `_render_decision_summary_hard_filters()` and `_render_decision_summary_next_actions()` so `_render_decision_summary()` reads as the document structure instead of inline list construction
  - centralized markdown artifact file validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer opens sign-off, decision, and evidence markdown files directly
  - centralized `demo_run_result.json` self-check metadata validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer owns status, path, operational approval, and artifact-list contract checks
  - split demo run artifact-check validation into `_require_demo_run_artifact_check()` so `validate_demo_run_result()` reads as artifact-check contract, evidence paths, and artifact-list validation
  - split demo run artifact-list validation into `_validate_demo_run_artifacts()` so `validate_demo_run_result()` keeps artifact-check, evidence path, and artifact-list checks at the same readable level
  - centralized package artifact file existence validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer owns output-directory and missing-artifact contract checks
  - centralized split JSON artifact loading and package-section match validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer owns local mapping/type wrappers for split artifact parity
  - centralized local package artifact validation in the service layer so artifact checking no longer loads the package document, validates coverage, iterates split JSON artifacts, or opens markdown artifacts directly
  - centralized artifact check success payload construction in the service layer so the CLI checker is now a thin wrapper around the local package evidence contract
  - centralized demo evidence receipt rendering in the service layer so demo execution no longer owns validation-summary lookup, receipt boundary text, or artifact inventory row rendering
  - split demo evidence receipt rendering into `_require_demo_receipt_context()`, `_load_demo_receipt_validation_summary()`, and `_build_demo_evidence_receipt_lines()` so `render_demo_evidence_receipt()` reads as context validation, line assembly, inventory rows, and final markdown join
  - split demo evidence file validation into `_validate_demo_artifact_inventory_for_evidence()` and `_validate_demo_receipt_for_evidence()` so `validate_demo_evidence_files()` keeps the checklist flow separate from inventory and receipt validation details
  - split demo evidence flag updates into `_mark_demo_evidence_checked()` so `validate_demo_evidence_files()` keeps validation calls separate from local evidence-check state mutation
  - centralized demo run result payload construction in the service layer so demo execution no longer owns evidence path, receipt path, artifact inventory, or field-order contract assembly
  - split demo run evidence summary assembly into `_build_demo_run_evidence_summary()` so `build_demo_run_result()` keeps demo identity separate from evidence paths, artifact check, and artifact inventory fields while preserving result JSON field order
  - split demo run export-derived fields into `_build_demo_run_export_fields()` so `build_demo_run_result()` keeps demo identity, evidence summary, exported artifact metadata, and clean-output state as separate ordered sections
  - split demo output directory preparation into `prepare_demo_output_dir()` so `run_demo()` shows output cleanup as a named setup step before seed, export, check, result build, and evidence write
  - split demo package export into `export_demo_decision_package()` so `run_demo()` shows seed, export, check, result build, and evidence write without repeating demo tenant/project constants inline
  - split demo run result construction into `_build_demo_run_result_from_export()` so `run_demo()` keeps demo identity constants and result field wiring out of the execution sequence
  - centralized demo evidence state persistence in the service layer so demo execution no longer repeats artifact-check assignment, JSON receipt writes, or evidence receipt writes
  - centralized demo and artifact-check failure payload construction in the service layer so CLI wrappers no longer duplicate failure JSON field order or error metadata
  - centralized the validated demo evidence self-check/write cycle in the service layer so demo execution now reads as seed, export, build result, and write validated evidence without dropping any evidence checkpoints
  - split validated demo evidence writes into initial, first-checked, receipt-ready, and final state helpers so `write_validated_demo_evidence()` reads as the local self-check sequence rather than repeated write calls
  - named the intermediate demo evidence checks with `_build_demo_artifact_check_for_first_written_result()` and `_build_demo_artifact_check_for_receipt_write()` so `write_validated_demo_evidence()` shows each self-check checkpoint without inline option bundles
  - centralized sample builder and sample validator failure payload construction in the service layer so local fixture CLIs no longer duplicate failure JSON field order or schema purpose strings
  - centralized the sample builder load/build/validate/write flow in the service layer so the builder CLI now delegates the whole package construction path instead of reassembling service calls
  - split CLI contract manifest derived summary assembly into `_build_cli_contract_manifest_contract_summary()` so validation result construction keeps manifest metadata separate from contract counts, case/script lists, required-field maps, and fingerprint generation
  - split CLI contract manifest per-case field validation into `_validate_cli_contract_manifest_contract_fields()` so each contract validation reads as shape check, case/script check, success/failure field contract check, and typed result return
  - split package section validation into `_validate_package_core_sections()` and `_validate_package_handoff_sections()` so the package validator separates decision/evidence sections from reviewer handoff, proposal handoff, signoff, audit, and export boundary sections
  - named smoke evidence context with `_SmokeEvidenceContext` so `check_smoke_result()` reads through output directory, evidence files, demo receipt, gate result, and recorded smoke result paths without positional tuple unpacking
  - split smoke result dependency validation into `_require_smoke_check_dependencies()` so `check_smoke_result()` reads as load smoke result, verify excluded actions, resolve evidence context, validate dependent artifacts, and build the check result
  - split smoke result checked-state ordering validation into `_require_smoke_result_checked_after_artifact_check()` so `_require_smoke_result_check_state()` separates fixed boolean field expectations from the artifact-before-smoke-check rule
  - split demo artifact-check validation into `_require_demo_artifact_check_for_smoke()` so `_require_demo_result_for_smoke()` keeps demo identity and result matching separate from nested artifact-check matching
  - split gate result loading and identity validation into `_load_gate_result_for_smoke()` so `_require_gate_result_for_smoke()` starts from a validated gate result before path, excluded-action, and artifact matching checks
  - split gate artifact-check validation into `_require_gate_artifact_check_for_smoke()` so `_require_gate_result_for_smoke()` keeps gate identity, paths, and excluded action checks separate from artifact field matching
  - split demo gate output assembly into `_require_gate_demo_evidence_paths()`, `_load_gate_demo_artifacts()`, and `_build_gate_demo_output_result()` so `gate_demo_output()` reads as artifact check, evidence-path validation, artifact-list loading, and final gate receipt construction
  - split smoke gate result metadata assembly into `_build_smoke_gate_run_metadata()` and `_build_smoke_gate_summary_metadata()` so `_build_smoke_gate_result()` keeps fixed wrapper fields separate from run identity, write/check flags, evidence flags, and final recommendation summary
  - split optional smoke gate result persistence into `_write_gate_result_when_requested()` so `smoke_demo_gate()` reads as run demo, gate artifacts, record gate result, build evidence files, and return smoke result without inline write branching
  - split smoke gate receipt construction into `_build_smoke_gate_result_from_demo()` so `smoke_demo_gate()` reads as demo execution, gate evaluation, optional gate persistence, and final smoke receipt return
  - split smoke gate evidence-file assembly into `_build_smoke_gate_evidence_files()` so `smoke_demo_gate()` no longer exposes demo result, demo receipt, and optional gate result path wiring inline
  - split smoke failure payload assembly into `_build_smoke_failure_paths()` and `_build_smoke_failure_state()` so `build_smoke_failure_result()` reads as fixed failure identity, evidence paths, write/check state, and exception fields while preserving failure JSON field order
  - split smoke check passed-state fields into `_build_smoke_check_state()` so `_build_smoke_check_result()` keeps fixed check identity separate from package/smoke verification flags
  - split smoke check metadata assembly into `_build_smoke_check_metadata()` so `_build_smoke_check_result()` keeps fixed check fields separate from smoke-derived tenant, project, artifact, recommendation, authorization, and clean-output fields
  - split sample decision package assembly into `_build_sample_decision_package()` plus section builders for unresolved gaps, opportunity reference, hard filters, soft-fit score, evidence summary, and bid-readiness checklist so `build_decision_package()` reads as validation and package assembly rather than inline fixture construction
  - split sample package core assembly into `_build_sample_package_core()` so `_build_sample_decision_package()` reads as core package fields plus package handoff artifacts while preserving the expected fixture package field order
  - split sample soft-fit factor assembly into `_build_sample_soft_fit_factors()` so `_build_sample_soft_fit_score()` keeps score and band separate from the ordered scoring evidence rows
  - split sample evidence summary assembly into `_build_sample_source_evidence()` and `_build_sample_missing_evidence()` so sample package construction separates positive source facts from unresolved evidence gaps without changing evidence order
  - split sample bid-readiness checklist item assembly into `_build_sample_bid_readiness_item()` so the checklist lists review tasks while the helper preserves owner, status, and required-before field order
  - split expected package sample metadata and opportunity reference parity into `_require_expected_package_sample_metadata()` and `_require_expected_package_opportunity_ref()` so sample expected-package validation reads as input validation, package contract validation, top-level parity, opportunity parity, and local coverage
  - split record decision package assembly into `_require_record_package_inputs()`, `_build_record_decision_package()`, and record section builders for opportunity reference, hard filters, soft-fit score, evidence summary, checklist, and unresolved gaps so `build_decision_package_from_record()` reads as project-record validation and package assembly
  - split record package core assembly into `_build_record_package_core()` so `_build_record_decision_package()` reads as core package fields plus package handoff artifacts while preserving the package field order contract
  - split record evidence summary assembly into `_build_record_source_evidence()`, `_build_record_score_summary_evidence()`, and `_build_record_missing_evidence()` so recommendation evidence, score fallback evidence, and missing-data gaps stay in the expected order without sharing one mixed branch
  - split package handoff artifact assembly into `_build_package_handoff_artifacts()` so sample and record package builders share reviewer handoff, proposal handoff, pending signoff, audit manifest, and export manifest construction without repeating package boundary artifacts
  - centralized the sample validator input checks and success payload construction in the service layer so the validator CLI no longer owns fixture-shape helpers or display-path formatting
  - split demo input validation into root, opportunity, capability profile, and operator notes helpers so `validate_demo_input()` reads as the local fixture contract sequence
  - moved sample validator regression tests onto service-owned helpers directly so tests no longer rely on the validator CLI re-exporting validation functions
  - moved artifact checker regression tests onto service-owned artifact check helpers directly so tests no longer treat the checker CLI as the validation owner
  - moved local demo seed fixture construction into the service layer so the demo runner no longer imports procurement schemas or the store directly
  - split local demo seed record construction into `_build_demo_decision_upsert()` and demo-section builders for opportunity, hard filters, score breakdown, checklist, and recommendation so `seed_demo_decision_record()` reads as store setup and deterministic upsert
  - moved project decision package export orchestration into the service layer so the export CLI no longer owns store lookup, default package output path selection, artifact write orchestration, or failure payload construction
  - split project decision package export result assembly into `_build_project_decision_package_export_result()` so export orchestration stays separate from the returned tenant/project receipt
  - moved local demo gate validation, gate result writing, and gate failure payload construction into the service layer so the gate CLI and smoke wrapper no longer import validation behavior from another script
  - moved local demo execution, smoke evidence-file ordering, smoke result construction, and smoke failure payload construction into the service layer so the demo and smoke CLIs now mainly handle argparse, persistence options, and stdout JSON
  - centralized smoke checker contract constants for smoke name, smoke check artifact name, evidence file field order, and excluded external runtime actions through the service layer
  - moved persisted smoke result validation, smoke check result writing, and smoke check failure payload construction into the service layer so the smoke checker CLI no longer imports artifact checker behavior or owns evidence validation rules
  - moved demo, gate, smoke, and artifact checker regression tests off script-module dynamic loading so service-owned helpers are tested directly and scripts are covered through subprocess CLI checks only
  - moved CLI contract manifest validator and validation-result checker regression tests off path-based dynamic loading so they import the script modules through normal Python module resolution while subprocess tests continue to prove CLI stdout and persistence behavior
  - moved CLI contract manifest validation-result checking, success field-order construction, write helper, and failure payload construction into the service layer so the result checker script now only handles argparse, current-manifest validation wiring, persistence options, and stdout JSON
  - moved CLI contract manifest validator schema/version/result-name constants, validation result field order, failure field order, failure payload construction, and validation-result writer into the service layer so the validator script no longer owns persisted receipt contract assembly
  - moved the CLI contract manifest validator body into the service layer, including stdout JSON contract checks, external action checks, case/script ordering, success/failure field contracts, and fingerprint construction; the validator script now remains as a thin CLI adapter with public compatibility aliases
  - moved CLI contract manifest validator regression tests onto service-owned validation directly so subprocess coverage is reserved for script stdout, handled failure, and receipt persistence behavior
  - removed the CLI contract manifest result checker script's dependency on the validator script; the checker now resolves the default manifest path locally and calls the service-owned manifest validator directly for persisted receipt checks
  - moved result-checker regression tests off validator-script helpers so in-process checks use service-owned validation while subprocess setup still exercises the validator CLI receipt writer
  - removed the sample builder script's dependency on the sample validator script by giving the builder its own local sample-input default path and limiting `sys.path` setup to the repository root
  - normalized smoke wrapper and smoke checker import setup so both CLI adapters add only the repository root to `sys.path`, matching the rest of the service-backed procurement package scripts
  - centralized local demo sample and CLI manifest relative paths in the service layer so sample validator, sample builder, manifest validator, and manifest result checker wrappers derive their default paths from one source of truth
  - removed remaining CLI contract regression test imports of validator/checker script modules; tests now use service-owned path and contract constants for in-process checks while subprocess coverage continues to exercise CLI behavior
  - normalized procurement decision package CLI wrapper root resolution to `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]` so every service-backed adapter uses the same repository-root import setup without carrying script-directory import state
  - centralized exception metadata rendering for service-owned failure payloads through `_exception_fields()` so CLI failure contract builders keep their field order while avoiding repeated mechanical `error_type` and `error` assembly
  - centralized optional path rendering through `_optional_path()` and named the recorded gate-result path in the smoke flow so evidence/result payload assembly reads as contract intent instead of repeated inline `str(...)` conditionals
  - named the smoke check contract through `SMOKE_CHECK_NAME` so success and failure receipt payloads share the same service-owned check identifier instead of repeating a string literal
  - named the smoke validation field groups for evidence paths, package artifact parity, demo identity, demo result parity, and demo artifact-check parity so `check_smoke_result()` reads as a sequence of contract checks rather than repeated inline tuples
  - centralized smoke result boolean flag validation through named required-true and required-false field groups so the checker states the contract before moving into evidence and artifact parity checks
  - named CLI contract manifest validation-result comparison field groups so the checker distinguishes case-map order validation from current-manifest parity validation without inline tuple noise
  - named CLI contract manifest result assembly helpers for case names, scripts, and case-field maps so validator output construction reads as contract projection rather than repeated comprehensions
  - named the CLI contract manifest identity values and validator helper so schema purpose, contract version, workflow, and local-only status are checked as one manifest identity contract
  - centralized exact ordered-value validation for external action lists so smoke and CLI manifest validators share the same missing/unknown/order comparison while preserving their existing error wording
  - centralized field-order projection through `_project_fields()` so evidence file maps and CLI manifest receipt payloads explicitly return their declared contract field order without repeating dict comprehensions
  - named the current-manifest fields copied into CLI contract validation-check receipts so persisted result checks project current validation state through an explicit field contract
  - split CLI manifest stdout contract validation into success and handled-failure section helpers so the validator reads as two receipt contracts while preserving exact field and message checks
  - split CLI manifest case script validation into its own helper so each contract entry reads as case identity, script contract, field contracts, and collection-level checks
  - split CLI manifest case success and failure field contract validation into dedicated helpers so each contract entry reads as case identity, script contract, success field contract, failure field contract, and collection-level checks
  - split CLI manifest contract collection validation into a dedicated helper so uniqueness, required case set, and case order checks are named separately from per-case validation
  - reused the ordered-value contract helper for smoke evidence file validation so missing, unknown, and order checks share the same field-order rule without repeating set arithmetic
  - reused the ordered-value contract helper for package artifact inventory and artifact-list validation so both paths express the same included-artifact order contract without repeating set arithmetic
  - reused the ordered-value contract helper inside exact string-list validators so CLI manifest lists, audit manifest lists, and export manifest lists share one readable missing, unknown, and order check
  - centralized duplicate-value validation through `_require_unique_values()` so artifact lists, smoke excluded actions, CLI manifest lists, and export/audit manifest lists state the no-duplicates contract by name
  - centralized string item collection through `_require_string_items()` so list-specific helpers keep their own top-level list contract while sharing the per-item non-empty string rule
  - made CLI contract non-empty string list validation reuse the shared string-list helper before applying its uniqueness contract
  - routed smoke excluded-action and CLI contract list validation through shared list helpers so the validators state only their domain-specific uniqueness and ordering contracts
  - let `_require_unique_values()` preserve specialized uniqueness messages so CLI contract case/script uniqueness can share the same helper without changing reader-facing errors
  - named CLI contract missing/extra case detail formatting so collection validation reads as uniqueness, case set, and order checks without inline message assembly
  - centralized missing and unknown value calculation through `_missing_values()` and `_unknown_values()` so mapping fields, ordered lists, and CLI case-set checks no longer repeat set arithmetic inline
  - centralized shared-value calculation through `_shared_values()` so CLI success forbidden-field checks read as an explicit overlap contract while failure required-field checks reuse `_missing_values()`
  - split package evidence type coverage into `_require_package_evidence_types()` so local package coverage validation names the required `source_fact` and `missing_evidence` contract directly
  - split package artifact file gap detection into `_missing_package_artifact_files()` so output directory validation separates directory existence from included-artifact file coverage
  - centralized marker gap detection through `_missing_markers()` so demo receipts, sign-off summaries, and evidence summaries share the same required-text scan while keeping their own error messages
  - split artifact inventory row construction into `_build_artifact_inventory_row_map()` so row rendering keeps included-artifact order while receipt validation can name the missing artifact directly
  - split artifact inventory file matching into `_require_artifact_inventory_file_match()` so the public validator names the inventory contract while the helper owns per-artifact SHA256 and size parity checks
  - routed `demo_run_result.artifact_check` boolean validation through `_require_boolean_fields()` so operational approval and checked-flag requirements share the same explicit true/false contract helper
  - split `demo_run_result` evidence path parity into `_require_demo_run_result_paths()` so artifact-check state validation stays separate from checked file and output-directory location checks
  - named `validation_summary.json` as `VALIDATION_SUMMARY_NAME` so artifact order, package-section mapping, audit grouping, receipt rendering, and receipt validation all refer to the same local evidence artifact name
  - split demo receipt artifact-table row extraction into `_artifact_inventory_rows_from_receipt()` so receipt validation reads as marker coverage, row contract, and validation-summary echo checks
  - named demo receipt validation-summary echo fields through `DEMO_RECEIPT_VALIDATION_SUMMARY_FIELDS` so receipt text checks state the operator summary and next review action contract directly
  - split demo receipt validation-summary echo checking into `_require_demo_receipt_validation_summary_fields()` so receipt validation delegates operator summary and next review action parity to a named contract helper
  - split demo evidence receipt path validation into `_require_demo_evidence_receipt_path()` so receipt file validation reads as path contract first, then receipt text contract
  - named demo evidence check flags through `DEMO_EVIDENCE_CHECK_FIELDS` and `_unchecked_demo_evidence_flags()` so missing demo evidence returns the same explicit unchecked contract without inline dict assembly
  - reused `DEMO_EVIDENCE_CHECK_FIELDS` across artifact checker, evidence gate, smoke wrapper, and smoke parity field contracts so demo evidence check ordering has one source of truth
  - projected demo evidence check flags through `_project_fields(..., DEMO_EVIDENCE_CHECK_FIELDS)` in gate and smoke result assembly so payload construction reuses the same named contract instead of copying each flag manually
  - wrapped demo evidence flag projection in `_project_demo_evidence_flags()` so gate and smoke payload assembly reads as evidence-state projection instead of generic field copying
  - named markdown artifact filenames for decision summary, evidence summary, bid-readiness checklist, and sign-off summary so artifact order, audit grouping, writer paths, and markdown validation share the same local evidence artifact names
  - named JSON artifact filenames for the decision package, reviewer handoff, proposal handoff, pending signoff, audit manifest, and export manifest so split-artifact maps, artifact order, audit grouping, loader paths, and writer paths use the same local evidence artifact names
  - split package artifact writing into `_write_package_json_artifacts()` and `_write_package_markdown_artifacts()` so `write_package_artifacts()` reads as validate package, prepare output directory, write JSON artifacts, write markdown artifacts, and return the write receipt
  - split package artifact write receipt assembly into `_build_package_artifact_write_result()` so `write_package_artifacts()` keeps persistence steps separate from the returned local completion receipt
  - split markdown artifact rendering into `_package_markdown_artifacts()` so markdown filenames and rendered text are assembled in one ordered map before file writes
  - split split-JSON artifact section path derivation into `_json_artifact_section_path()` so artifact validation names the error-path contract instead of carrying suffix manipulation inline
  - named decision package validation path labels through `DECISION_PACKAGE_DOCUMENT_PATH` and `DECISION_PACKAGE_ROOT_PATH` so split-artifact validation and package coverage checks share the same reader-facing error path contract
  - routed validation path message rewriting through `PACKAGE_DOCUMENT_VALIDATION_PATH` and `_validation_path_message()` so full-document and split-section validators share one reader-facing error-path translation helper
  - split package document validation into `_require_package_document_root()`, `_require_package_document_package()`, `_require_package_identity()`, and `_validate_package_sections()` so `validate_package_document()` reads as root contract, package contract, identity contract, and section validation
  - routed lower-case boolean rendering through `_bool_label()` so markdown receipts and validation messages share the same simple true/false label formatting
  - made smoke evidence file validation return `Path` values through `_require_existing_file_path()` so existence checks, JSON loading, and payload string rendering each happen at the point where they belong
  - grouped smoke field parity checks through `_require_matching_fields()` so package artifact, demo result, demo artifact-check, and gate comparisons read as named contract checks instead of repeated loops
  - named smoke demo context parity through `SMOKE_DEMO_CONTEXT_FIELDS` so output directory and clean-output checks sit beside identity and result parity contracts
  - split gate result path parity into `_require_gate_result_paths()` so smoke result checking reads as gate identity, gate evidence paths, and excluded-action parity
  - split excluded external action parity into `_require_matching_excluded_external_actions()` so smoke and gate boundary lists are validated and compared through one explicit contract helper
  - split excluded external action order validation into `_require_excluded_external_action_order()` so smoke/gate boundary list extraction and runtime-action order matching read separately
  - split gate result identity validation into `_require_gate_result_identity()` so gate name/status checks are separated from path and boundary parity checks
  - split smoke gate success receipt assembly into `_build_smoke_gate_result()` so `smoke_demo_gate()` reads as demo execution, gate persistence, evidence-file mapping, and final receipt construction
  - split smoke result identity and check-state validation into `_require_smoke_result_identity()` and `_require_smoke_result_check_state()` so smoke checking starts with named receipt contracts before reading evidence files
  - split smoke evidence file field validation into `_require_evidence_file_fields()` so evidence-file mapping and required field order checks read separately
  - grouped smoke evidence path parity through `_require_matching_evidence_paths()` so smoke result evidence-file checks read as one receipt path contract
  - grouped required smoke evidence file path extraction through `_require_smoke_evidence_file_paths()` so demo, receipt, gate, and smoke result files are checked before downstream package and gate validation
  - split single smoke evidence file path extraction into `_require_smoke_evidence_file_path()` so repeated evidence-file existence checks read as one reusable field contract
  - split checked smoke result path parity into `_require_checked_smoke_result_path()` so the current receipt file check is separated from evidence file extraction
  - split recorded smoke check result validation into `_require_recorded_smoke_check_result()` so persisted check-result parity is checked after the current result is assembled
  - split recorded smoke check result written-state, file, and payload validation into `_require_smoke_check_result_written()`, `_require_smoke_check_result_file()`, and `_require_smoke_check_result_payload()` so persisted receipt checks read in execution order
  - split smoke check result payload assembly into `_build_smoke_check_result()` so `check_smoke_result()` validates all evidence first, then builds the current receipt before optional persisted-result parity
  - split smoke check evidence-file payload assembly into `_build_smoke_check_evidence_files()` so nested evidence paths are built separately from the top-level check receipt
  - split smoke check orchestration into `_load_smoke_result_for_check()`, `_require_smoke_evidence_context()`, `_require_smoke_artifact_check()`, `_require_demo_result_for_smoke()`, and `_require_gate_result_for_smoke()` so `check_smoke_result()` reads as the local receipt check sequence
  - split CLI contract validation-result checking into identity, current-manifest parity, and check-result assembly helpers so persisted manifest validation receipts are checked before the current check receipt is built
  - split CLI contract validation-result loading and manifest-path extraction into `_load_cli_contract_manifest_validation_result()` and `_manifest_path_from_cli_contract_validation_result()` so persisted receipt checking starts from a validated result before current manifest comparison
  - split CLI contract manifest validation receipt assembly into `_build_cli_contract_manifest_validation_result()` so manifest validation reads as load, validate, extract contract facts, and build the receipt
  - split CLI contract manifest loading and derived fact validation into `_load_cli_contract_manifest()` and `_validate_cli_contract_manifest_facts()` so `validate_cli_contract_manifest()` reads as load content, validate facts, and build the validation receipt
  - split CLI contract fingerprint payload assembly into `_build_cli_contract_manifest_fingerprint_payload()`, `_build_cli_contract_stdout_fingerprint_payload()`, and `_build_cli_contract_entry_fingerprint_payloads()` so the hash function only encodes and hashes a named payload
  - replaced repeated CLI contract case/script derivation with `_CliContractManifestContractsResult` so contract validation returns contracts, case names, and scripts from the same checked collection pass
  - replaced CLI contract validation tuple unpacking with `_CliContractManifestContractResult` so validated contract, case name, and script values are carried by name through collection validation
  - split per-contract manifest validation into `_validate_cli_contract_manifest_contract()` so the contract collection validator gathers validated case/script facts before checking collection-level uniqueness and order
  - split CLI contract case-name set and order checks into `_require_cli_contract_case_set()` and `_require_cli_contract_case_order()` so missing/extra case validation stays separate from deterministic case ordering
  - split CLI contract external-action order validation into `_require_cli_contract_external_action_order()` so excluded action list extraction and required order matching read separately
  - split CLI contract collection validation into `_require_cli_contract_collection_unique_values()` and `_require_cli_contract_case_set_and_order()` so duplicate checks stay separate from required case set/order checks
  - split CLI contract case-script validation into `_require_cli_contract_repo_script_path()` and `_require_cli_contract_expected_case_script()` so repo path existence and case-to-script mapping read separately
  - split CLI stdout success and failure contracts into identity and payload-field helpers so exit/status checks stay separate from forbidden/required field-list rules
  - split CLI contract expected case-field lookup into `_require_cli_contract_expected_case_fields()` so per-case contract resolution stays separate from exact field-list validation
  - split success-field manifest rules into `_require_cli_contract_success_fields()` so exact field-list matching is followed by named success payload requirements
  - split success-field status and forbidden-field checks into `_require_cli_contract_success_status_field()` and `_require_cli_contract_success_forbidden_fields()` so required success payload fields and rejected error fields are named separately
  - split failure-field manifest rules into `_require_cli_contract_failure_fields()` so exact field-list matching is followed by named failure payload requirements
  - split failure-field required payload checks into `_require_cli_contract_failure_required_fields()` so global failure fields are verified by a named contract
  - split CLI contract validation-result parity into case-map order and current-field match helpers so persisted/current manifest comparisons show their two separate contracts
  - split opportunity reference field validation into `_require_opportunity_ref_fields()` so field order and required reference values read as separate contracts
  - split hard-filter item and status validation into `_validate_hard_filter_item()` and `_require_hard_filter_status()` so list iteration, item shape, and reviewed status checks read separately
  - split soft-fit score value, band, and factor-list validation into `_require_soft_fit_score_value()`, `_require_soft_fit_score_band()`, and `_require_soft_fit_score_factors()` so the score block reads as ordered scoring contracts
  - split soft-fit factor validation into `_validate_soft_fit_factor()` so score-level validation stays separate from repeated factor item shape checks
  - split soft-fit factor identity, score, and evidence-link validation into `_require_soft_fit_factor_identity()`, `_require_soft_fit_factor_score()`, and `_require_soft_fit_factor_evidence_links()` so each factor reads as named scoring evidence
  - routed repeated package list validation through `_validate_list_items()` so soft-fit factors, evidence summary rows, and bid-readiness checklist rows share the same list and item-path contract without repeating loop mechanics
  - split evidence summary item validation into `_validate_evidence_summary_item()` so list-level validation stays separate from repeated evidence fact shape checks
  - split evidence summary item identity, type, and text validation into `_require_evidence_summary_identity()`, `_require_evidence_summary_type()`, and `_require_evidence_summary_text_fields()` so evidence rows read as ordered facts
  - split bid-readiness checklist item validation into `_validate_bid_readiness_checklist_item()` so checklist list validation stays separate from repeated item shape and status checks
  - split bid-readiness checklist item text and status validation into `_require_bid_readiness_checklist_text_fields()` and `_require_bid_readiness_checklist_status()` so item shape reads as field order, text facts, and reviewed status
  - split validation-summary status validation into `_require_validation_summary_status()` so schema and boundary status fields are checked before wording and gap contracts
  - split validation-summary boundary checks into `_require_non_approval_summary()` and `_require_scoped_review_action()` so non-approval and scoped-review wording contracts are named explicitly
  - split validation-summary boundary and unresolved-gap validation into `_require_validation_summary_boundary()` and `_require_validation_summary_unresolved_gaps()` so status fields stay separate from local review boundary facts
  - split handoff and audit non-authorization note validation into `_require_non_authorization_note()` so reviewer, proposal, and audit validators share one boundary wording contract
  - routed reviewer, proposal, and audit non-authorization note field extraction through `_require_non_authorization_note_field()` so each boundary validator states only its section-level contract
  - split reviewer handoff validation into `_require_reviewer_handoff_request()` and `_require_reviewer_handoff_boundary()` so request fields and non-authorization wording are checked separately
  - split audit manifest identity validation into `_require_audit_manifest_identity()` so schema purpose, packet status, package ID, and recommendation parity are checked before artifact and boundary contracts
  - split audit manifest artifact inventory validation into `_require_audit_manifest_artifact_inventory()` so included-artifact and grouped-artifact contracts are checked before excluded actions and boundary wording
  - split audit manifest local boundary validation into `_require_audit_manifest_boundary()` so excluded actions and non-authorization wording are checked after artifact inventory
  - routed repeated included-artifact and excluded-action field validation through `_require_included_artifacts_field()` and `_require_excluded_actions_field()` so proposal, audit, and export validators reuse the same ordered-list contract by field name
  - split proposal handoff identity validation into `_require_proposal_handoff_identity()` so scope, source package, and recommendation parity are checked before readiness and boundary lists
  - split proposal handoff readiness validation into `_require_proposal_handoff_readiness()` so required inputs, blocked-until parity, and drafting status are checked as one named contract
  - split proposal handoff boundary validation into `_require_proposal_handoff_boundary()` so allowed next steps, excluded actions, and non-authorization wording are checked after readiness
  - split pending signoff review-boundary validation into `_require_pending_signoff_review_boundary()` so pending status, reviewer, review-only scope, and false operational approval are checked as one named contract
  - split pending signoff review identity and operational boundary validation into `_require_pending_signoff_review_identity()` and `_require_pending_signoff_operational_boundary()` so pending reviewer facts stay separate from non-operational-approval safeguards
  - split export manifest inventory validation into `_require_export_manifest_inventory()` so export artifact order and excluded-action order are checked as one named handoff contract
  - retained local-only authorization boundaries for reviewer handoff, proposal handoff, pending signoff, audit manifest, and export manifest without running external systems
